body.title: Consequence check: ADC queue rack device shipped
body.result: No double-dequeue events in recent channel log; /query-ticket working; no unhealthy device reports. Gate date 2026-06-08 not yet reached but all observable signals clear now.
body.description:
**What changed:** A rack device in UU now serves the work ticket queue via MCP; cc_queue.py is a backend implementation detail; claiming is gone.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Any consumer that bypasses MCP and reads cc_queue.py directly will still work but won't benefit from atomic in_progress marking — could produce double-dequeue if two consumers race. (2) If the queue device is down, all ticket access is blocked; prior cc_queue.py was always available (no device dependency). Single point of failure introduced. (3) Device startup time adds latency to first queue_next call after a rack restart. (4) The skills-sync rsync copy means skill files exist in two places — MCP path switch must land in both ~/.claude/skills/ and the cc_skills/ repo mirror or they'll diverge.
**Signals to watch:** Double-dequeue log entries (same ticket in_progress twice); queue device appearing as unhealthy in rack_health; /query-ticket returning errors after rack restart; skill mirror divergence between ~/.claude/skills/ and cc_skills/.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-08.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
